wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the MissLost project overview section focused on the lost and found community platform for University of Economics and Technology (UEH). Explain its purpose as a blog web application for reporting lost items and finding found items, architecture overview, and relationship to university campus community. Document the core functionality including item posting, AI-powered matching, real-time chat, storage management, and training points system. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase. Provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases including item registration, matching process, and handover procedures. Document the system's role in campus life and community building.

wiki_catalogs.name: Introduction and Purpose
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the MissLost project introduction and purpose section focused on the lost and found community platform for University of Economics and Technology (UEH). Explain the platform's mission to connect students and staff who have lost items with those who have found them. Document the problem it solves in campus community life, including the challenges of item recovery, communication gaps, and lack of centralized tracking systems. Describe the target audience including students, faculty, and staff members. Include the platform's role in building community trust, reducing administrative burden, and enhancing campus safety. Provide context about the university environment and how the platform fits into campus life. Use clear, accessible language for stakeholders unfamiliar with the project.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Shell
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the MissLost frontend application shell. Explain the root layout structure, provider hierarchy, and global configuration setup. Document the ThemeProvider implementation for dark/light mode switching, RouteGuard for authentication protection, and ClientShell for client-side hydration. Detail the Next.js App Router integration, metadata configuration, and font loading strategy. Include the component provider chain, global CSS imports, and internationalization setup. Provide examples of how these foundational components work together to create the application's base structure and explain the relationship between layout.tsx and its child components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for authentication endpoints. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas for user registration (/auth/register), login (/auth/login), logout (/auth/logout), email verification (/auth/verify-email), forgot password (/auth/forgot-password), reset password (/auth/reset-password), Google OAuth login (/auth/google), and Google OAuth callback (/auth/google/callback). Include JWT token management, authentication headers, error handling strategies, and security considerations. Provide request/response examples with JSON schemas, authentication flow diagrams, and client implementation guidelines for each endpoint.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Bootstrap
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the MissLost backend application bootstrap process. Explain the main entry point configuration, module orchestration, and global providers setup. Document the NestJS application initialization sequence, dependency injection container configuration, and global middleware registration. Detail the application service functionality, controller setup, and how the modular architecture is initialized. Include practical examples of the bootstrapping process, configuration options, and startup sequence. Address common initialization issues and debugging approaches. Explain the relationship between AppModule and individual feature modules in the bootstrap process.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system design documentation for the MissLost platform. Describe the overall system architecture as a full-stack web application with clear separation between frontend and backend layers. Document the modular monolithic architecture pattern used in NestJS backend where each feature is organized into independent modules. Explain the frontend architecture using Next.js App Router with page-based routing and component composition. Detail the data flow patterns from user interface interactions through API endpoints to database operations. Include system boundaries showing how different components interact, the request-response cycle, and real-time communication patterns. Document the separation of concerns between presentation layer, business logic layer, and data persistence. Address scalability considerations, performance optimization strategies, and fault tolerance mechanisms. Provide architectural diagrams illustrating the complete system flow and component interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the User Authentication & Authorization system focused on the platform's security and access control mechanisms. Document the dual authentication approach using JWT tokens and Google OAuth integration, explaining the registration process, login workflows, and session management. Detail the role-based access control system with user roles and permissions, including admin privileges and standard user limitations. Explain the JWT strategy implementation, token refresh mechanisms, and security considerations. Cover the Google OAuth integration setup, callback handling, and account linking processes. Document the user entity structure, password hashing, and credential validation. Include practical examples of authentication flows, error handling scenarios, and security best practices. Address common authentication issues and troubleshooting steps for both developers and users.

wiki_catalogs.name: Feed System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the feed system page that displays lost and found posts in a unified timeline. Document the post listing implementation, filtering and sorting mechanisms, and real-time updates for new posts. Explain the data fetching patterns from the backend API, pagination handling, and infinite scroll implementation. Detail the post card components, status indicators, and visual presentation of different post types. Include the loading state implementation with skeleton screens, error handling for failed API calls, and user interaction patterns for post navigation. Provide examples of how users can filter posts by status, category, and location, and document the integration with the AI matching system for suggested matches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Chat System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the ChatWindow component that displays real-time conversations. Document the message rendering logic, user identification, message styling, and conversation header functionality. Explain how the component handles different message types (text, image), timestamps, read receipts, and sender differentiation. Detail the scroll behavior for continuous message history, infinite scrolling for older messages, and smooth scrolling to new messages. Include examples of message formatting, avatar display, and conversation metadata presentation. Address performance considerations for large message histories and memory management for optimal user experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Overview Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the admin overview dashboard system. Document the real-time statistics display including total users, active lost/found posts, pending reviews, handovers, and storage items. Explain the enhanced statistics with post status breakdown, top categories visualization, recent activity feed, and user registration tracking. Detail the data fetching mechanisms using the /admin/dashboard and /admin/dashboard/enhanced endpoints. Include the bento grid layout components, animated progress bars, color-coded status indicators, and responsive design patterns. Provide examples of the dashboard card components, data visualization techniques, and real-time data updates. Address the loading states, error handling, and performance optimization for dashboard rendering.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Registration and Login
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the user registration and login functionality in the MissLost authentication system. Detail the registration process including email validation, password hashing with bcrypt, user creation workflow, and email verification token generation. Document the login process with password comparison, account status validation, and JWT token generation. Include concrete examples from the codebase showing DTO validation schemas, error handling patterns, and user entity structure. Explain the integration with Supabase for user data persistence, password security measures, and account lifecycle management. Address common registration/login issues, security considerations for password handling, and best practices for user onboarding.

wiki_catalogs.name: Chat Infrastructure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Chat Infrastructure component. Thoroughly explain the WebSocket-based messaging system, real-time communication architecture, and conversation management. Document the chat service implementation including message persistence, conversation threading, and user presence tracking. Detail the controller endpoints for chat operations, DTO validation schemas, and entity relationships. Include concrete examples of chat workflows such as creating conversations, sending messages, and managing chat history. Document WebSocket connection handling, message broadcasting patterns, and real-time update mechanisms. Explain integration with Supabase real-time features, connection pooling, and scalability considerations for concurrent chat sessions. Address common chat issues like message ordering, offline message handling, and connection recovery strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lost Posts System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Lost Posts System covering the complete lifecycle of lost item reporting. Document the post creation workflow including form validation, image uploads, and metadata collection. Explain the approval process with review criteria, admin verification steps, and status transitions (pending, approved, rejected, completed). Detail the query system with filtering options, search capabilities, and pagination. Document the relationship with AI matching algorithms for finding potential matches. Include concrete examples of POST /lost-posts creation, PUT /lost-posts/:id updates, and GET /lost-posts queries. Explain the integration with storage system for item tracking and handover process. Address common issues like duplicate submissions, missing information, and approval delays.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lost Posts Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Lost Posts Management system focused on the complete workflow for reporting lost items. Document the post creation process including form fields, validation rules, and required metadata such as item descriptions, locations, dates, and photos. Explain the approval workflow with admin review processes, status management (pending, approved, rejected), and automated notifications to users. Detail the post lifecycle from initial submission through completion, including status transitions, audit trails, and user communication. Cover the frontend components for lost post creation, editing, and management, including form validation and user feedback mechanisms. Document the integration with the AI matching system for finding potential matches with found posts. Include examples of different lost post scenarios, common item types, and administrative oversight procedures. Address moderation workflows, spam prevention, and content quality assurance measures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Admin Overview Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Admin Overview Dashboard system focused on the central administrative interface and analytics monitoring. Document the dashboard layout, key performance indicators, and system health metrics displayed to administrators. Explain the real-time statistics panels showing post counts, user activity, match success rates, and platform usage trends. Detail the administrative controls available for quick system oversight, including emergency shutdown capabilities, system status monitoring, and alert management. Cover the integration with backend analytics services and data visualization components. Document the filtering and reporting capabilities for different time periods and user segments. Include examples of dashboard customization options, metric thresholds, and automated alert configurations. Address the security considerations for dashboard access and the protection of sensitive administrative data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the MissLost application. Include prerequisites and system requirements, development environment setup with Node.js, PostgreSQL, and Docker configuration. Document database setup with required extensions (pg_trgm, uuid-ossp, vector), environment variables configuration for Supabase, JWT, and AI services. Provide step-by-step instructions for running the application locally using Docker Compose. Include initial project structure walkthrough, basic usage examples for user registration, post creation, and matching process. Document common setup issues and troubleshooting steps. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the MissLost core features section focused on the main functionalities that define the platform. Document the primary features including user authentication and authorization, lost item posting system, found item submission, AI-powered matching algorithms, real-time chat communication, storage management integration, training points system, and administrative oversight. For each feature, explain the user workflow, business logic, and technical implementation approach. Include the approval workflow for posts, category classification system, manual override capabilities, notification system, and handover process. Provide concrete examples of how users interact with each feature and how administrators manage content. Document the integration points between features and explain how they work together to create a seamless user experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Pages
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the authentication system pages. Document the login form implementation with email/password validation, registration form with user input validation, and Google OAuth callback handling. Explain the authentication flow from initial login to successful user session establishment. Include form validation patterns, error handling strategies, and redirect mechanisms after authentication. Detail the Google OAuth integration setup, callback processing, and user session management. Provide examples of form submission handling, loading states, and user feedback mechanisms. Address security considerations for authentication flows and state management during the login process.

wiki_catalogs.name: Posts Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for lost and found posts management endpoints. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas for CRUD operations on lost posts (/lost-posts/*) and found posts (/found-posts/*). Include post creation, approval workflows, status management, filtering capabilities, and search functionality. Document request validation, image upload handling, post approval processes, and status transition endpoints. Provide examples of post creation payloads, approval workflows, and common query parameters for filtering and sorting posts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the MissLost authentication system. Thoroughly explain JWT token management, Google OAuth integration, and role-based access control implementation. Document the authentication service functionality, user entity structure, and security strategies. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing login/register workflows, token refresh mechanisms, and user session management. Document DTO validation schemas, guard implementations, and decorator usage patterns. Explain relationships with Supabase authentication, password hashing, and user permission systems. Address common authentication issues, security considerations, and best practices for token handling and user management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed technology stack documentation for the MissLost platform. Document the complete technology ecosystem including backend technologies (NestJS 11.0.1, TypeScript, PostgreSQL 15+, Supabase, Redis), frontend technologies (Next.js 16.2.3, React, Tailwind CSS, Material Design), and supporting technologies (Docker, pg_trgm, vector extensions, YOLO-based AI detection). Explain the rationale behind technology choices, highlighting how each component addresses specific requirements like real-time communication, AI integration, and authentication. Detail version compatibility requirements, dependency management strategies, and upgrade paths. Include performance characteristics, scalability implications, and maintenance considerations for each technology. Document development tools, build processes, and deployment configurations. Address licensing considerations and long-term sustainability of technology choices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lost & Found Posting System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Lost & Found Posting System focused on the core content creation and management functionality. Document the complete workflow for creating lost and found posts, including form validation, image uploads, and metadata collection. Explain the approval workflow with admin review processes, status management (pending, approved, rejected), and user notifications. Detail the category classification system, item description analysis, and automatic categorization features. Cover the post lifecycle from creation to completion, including status transitions and audit trails. Document the user interface components for post creation, editing, and management. Explain the integration with the AI matching system and how posts are processed for potential matches. Include examples of different post types, common use cases, and administrative oversight procedures. Address moderation workflows, spam prevention, and content quality assurance measures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lost Items Section
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the lost items section page that handles lost item reporting and management. Document the lost post creation form, including field validation, image upload functionality, and category selection. Explain the approval workflow integration, status tracking, and user feedback mechanisms. Detail the form submission process, error handling for validation failures, and success confirmation messages. Include the integration with the AI categorization system for automatic item classification and the manual override capabilities for administrators. Provide examples of form components, validation rules, and the user journey from post creation to approval status changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Conversation List
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the ConversationList component that manages the sidebar navigation of active conversations. Document the conversation selection logic, last message preview, unread message indicators, and participant information display. Explain the conversation filtering, sorting by recency, and search functionality if implemented. Detail the conversation item rendering, hover effects, and selection states. Include examples of conversation metadata display, participant avatars, and timestamp formatting. Address performance optimization for large conversation lists, virtual scrolling if implemented, and efficient re-rendering when conversations change.

wiki_catalogs.name: Post Management Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the post management interface used by administrators for content moderation. Document the post listing system with filtering by status (pending, approved, rejected, matched, closed), post type (lost/found), and search functionality. Explain the moderation workflow including approve/reject actions, status updates, and bulk operations. Detail the post preview components showing title, description, images, user information, and category details. Include the approval process with automatic AI matching triggers, notification system integration, and status change tracking. Provide examples of the moderation interface, post management controls, and administrative decision-making workflows. Address the integration with AI matching system, user notifications, and audit trail logging for all moderation actions.

wiki_catalogs.name: JWT Token Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for JWT token management in the MissLost authentication system. Explain JWT strategy implementation, token signing and verification processes, and payload structure containing user identity and role information. Document the JWT authentication guard functionality, protected route access control, and token validation middleware. Include concrete examples showing token generation during login, token refresh mechanisms, and current user extraction using decorators. Detail the integration between JWT strategy and NestJS guards, token lifecycle management, and security considerations for token storage and transmission. Address token expiration handling, refresh token implementation, and best practices for secure token management in the frontend and backend.

wiki_catalogs.name: Notification System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Notification System component. Thoroughly explain the event-driven notification architecture, notification types, and delivery mechanisms. Document the notification service implementation including event triggering, notification templates, and user preference management. Detail the controller endpoints for notification operations, subscription management, and notification history. Include concrete examples of notification workflows such as match notifications, post status changes, and system alerts. Document integration with Supabase real-time subscriptions, notification queuing strategies, and delivery confirmation mechanisms. Explain notification filtering by user preferences, batch processing for high-volume notifications, and retry mechanisms for failed deliveries. Address common notification issues like duplicate notifications, delivery failures, and performance optimization for notification scaling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Found Posts System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Found Posts System covering the complete lifecycle of found item reporting. Document the post creation workflow including item description, location details, and submission procedures. Explain the approval process with verification steps, admin review criteria, and status management. Detail the query system with filtering by location, date, and item type. Document the relationship with AI matching algorithms for connecting found items with lost posts. Include concrete examples of POST /found-posts creation, PUT /found-posts/:id updates, and GET /found-posts queries. Explain the integration with chat system for communication between finder and owner. Address common issues like false positives, incomplete descriptions, and location verification challenges.

wiki_catalogs.name: Found Posts Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Found Posts Management system focused on the complete workflow for reporting found items. Document the post creation process including form fields, validation rules, and required metadata such as item descriptions, locations found, dates, and photos. Explain the approval workflow with admin review processes, status management (pending, approved, rejected), and automated notifications to users. Detail the post lifecycle from initial submission through completion, including status transitions, audit trails, and user communication. Cover the frontend components for found post creation, editing, and management, including form validation and user feedback mechanisms. Document the integration with the AI matching system for finding potential matches with lost posts. Include examples of different found post scenarios, common item types, and administrative oversight procedures. Address moderation workflows, spam prevention, and content quality assurance measures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Post Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Post Management System focused on the administrative controls for content moderation and post lifecycle management. Document the post listing interface with filtering options for status, category, date ranges, and user reports. Explain the content moderation workflows including approval processes, rejection procedures, and automated flagging systems. Detail the post editing capabilities for administrative corrections, content removal, and status updates. Cover the user action controls including post suspension, deletion, and appeal handling. Document the integration with AI matching systems for post verification and the manual override capabilities for administrative decisions. Include examples of common moderation scenarios, decision trees for content approval, and escalation procedures for disputed posts. Address the audit trail functionality for tracking all administrative actions and maintaining transparency in content management decisions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the MissLost backend system. Describe the NestJS-based architecture, modular design patterns, and system boundaries. Document component interactions, data flows, and integration patterns including Supabase authentication, PostgreSQL database, and AI matching services. Explain technical decisions, trade-offs, and constraints. Include infrastructure requirements, scalability considerations, and deployment topology. Provide system context diagrams and component breakdowns. Address cross-cutting concerns like security, monitoring, and error handling. Document technology stack including NestJS ecosystem, PostgreSQL with extensions, Redis caching, and external service integrations. Explain the plugin-based module system and dependency injection patterns used throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the MissLost platform architecture overview. Describe the high-level system design as a full-stack web application with NestJS backend and Next.js frontend. Document the modular architecture pattern used throughout the application, explaining how features are organized into independent modules. Detail the technology stack including PostgreSQL database with advanced extensions, Supabase authentication and real-time features, Redis caching, and AI detection integration. Explain the deployment architecture using Docker containers and the orchestration strategy. Include system boundaries, component interactions, and data flow patterns. Document the separation of concerns between frontend presentation layer, backend API layer, and database layer. Address cross-cutting concerns like authentication, authorization, logging, and error handling. Provide architectural diagrams showing the overall system structure and component relationships.

wiki_catalogs.name: Main Application Pages
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the core user-facing pages of the MissLost application. Document the feed system showing lost and found posts with filtering capabilities, the lost item posting interface, the found item submission process, and user post management. Explain the page routing, data fetching patterns, and real-time updates for post listings. Include the loading states implementation, error handling across different page contexts, and user interaction patterns. Detail the integration with backend APIs for post operations, status management, and approval workflows. Provide examples of form handling, data validation, and user feedback mechanisms specific to each page type.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Matching API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for AI matching endpoints. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, and request/response schemas for text similarity matching, YOLO-based object categorization, and matching result management. Include endpoints for triggering AI analysis, retrieving matching results, manual override capabilities, and admin controls for matching approval. Document AI algorithm integration, similarity scoring mechanisms, and result filtering options. Provide examples of matching workflows, result interpretation, and integration patterns with the posts management system.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the User Management system. Detail user entity relationships, field definitions, and training points allocation. Document primary/foreign keys, indexes, and constraints for user profiles and permissions. Explain data validation rules, profile management workflows, and training points calculation algorithms. Include database schema diagrams and sample user data. Document user service methods, profile update operations, and role-based access patterns. Specify user lifecycle management, account status handling, and administrative controls. Address user data security, privacy requirements, and access control mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment architecture documentation for the MissLost platform. Document the containerized deployment strategy using Docker Compose for orchestrating multiple services including backend API, frontend application, PostgreSQL database, Redis cache, and Supabase authentication. Explain the multi-stage build process for both backend and frontend applications, including optimization strategies and production configurations. Detail the environment variable management, secrets handling, and configuration deployment patterns. Document the scaling strategies, load balancing approaches, and health check implementations. Include deployment pipelines, CI/CD considerations, and rollback procedures. Address security considerations in production deployments, network configurations, and data backup strategies. Provide monitoring and logging setup recommendations, performance tuning guidelines, and capacity planning considerations for different deployment scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI-Powered Matching System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the AI-Powered Matching System focused on the intelligent algorithms that connect lost and found items. Document the dual matching approach combining text similarity analysis and YOLO-based object detection for item categorization. Explain the similarity scoring algorithms, threshold configurations, and manual override capabilities. Detail the integration with the categories system and how AI determines item classifications. Cover the matching workflow from post creation through successful connections, including notification systems and user feedback loops. Document the performance metrics, accuracy measurements, and continuous improvement processes. Explain the machine learning pipeline, training data requirements, and model updating procedures. Include examples of successful matches, common matching scenarios, and edge cases. Address the balance between automated matching and human oversight, including appeal processes and administrator intervention capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Found Items Section
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the found items section page that manages found item submissions and matching processes. Document the found post creation interface, including item description forms, image upload capabilities, and category assignment. Explain the AI matching integration for automatically suggesting potential owners based on post similarities. Detail the approval workflow, status management, and communication features with potential owners. Include the matching algorithm implementation, user notification system for matches, and the handover process coordination. Provide examples of the form components, matching visualization, and the complete user flow from found item submission to successful handover.

wiki_catalogs.name: Message Input
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the MessageInput component that handles user message composition and submission. Document the text input functionality, image attachment capabilities, emoji support, and message validation. Explain the send button behavior, keyboard shortcuts, auto-resize functionality, and input state management. Detail the optimistic UI pattern implementation, error handling for failed sends, and retry mechanisms. Include examples of rich text editing, file upload integration, and real-time validation feedback. Address accessibility features, mobile responsiveness, and input performance optimization for smooth user experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the user management interface enabling administrative control over platform users. Document the user listing system with sorting by registration date, role, status, and training points. Explain the user action controls including role assignment (admin, storage_staff, regular_user), account status management (active, suspended, pending_verify), and bulk user operations. Detail the user profile viewing capabilities showing registration details, training points history, post activity, and account creation date. Include the user search and filtering functionality, role-based access control implementation, and administrative audit trails. Provide examples of user management workflows, role assignment processes, and account status changes. Address the integration with training points system, user activity monitoring, and compliance with university regulations for student accounts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Google OAuth Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Google OAuth integration in the MissLost authentication system. Explain the Google OAuth strategy implementation, profile retrieval process, and social login workflow. Include concrete examples showing OAuth callback handling, user attribute extraction, and seamless integration with the existing user management system. Detail the setup requirements, client configuration, and security considerations for OAuth implementation. Address user synchronization between Google profiles and local user accounts, handling of missing profile information, and error scenarios in OAuth authentication.

wiki_catalogs.name: Categories System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Categories System covering item classification and categorization functionality. Document the category hierarchy, parent-child relationships, and classification rules. Explain the AI-based categorization integration with YOLO object detection and text similarity algorithms. Detail the manual override capabilities for administrators and category management workflows. Document the category entity structure, relationships with posts, and indexing strategies. Include concrete examples of category creation, assignment during post creation, and dynamic categorization updates. Explain the integration with AI matching system for improved post pairing accuracy. Address common issues like category ambiguity, misclassification errors, and category maintenance procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Category Classification System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Category Classification System focused on item categorization and classification functionality. Explain the category entity structure, relationships, and metadata management. Detail the integration with YOLO-based object detection for automatic item classification and manual override capabilities for administrators. Cover the frontend components for category selection during post creation, including dynamic category loading and user-friendly categorization interfaces. Document the category management system for administrators, including creation, modification, and deletion of categories. Explain the relationship between categories and posts, including category assignment during post creation and category-based filtering. Include examples of different item types, categorization scenarios, and administrative oversight procedures for maintaining category accuracy and completeness.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Interactions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed component interaction documentation for the MissLost platform architecture. Document the interaction patterns between frontend components and backend services, including HTTP request flows, WebSocket connections for real-time features, and API gateway patterns. Explain the module-to-module communication in the NestJS backend, dependency injection patterns, and service layer interactions. Detail the data exchange protocols between frontend and backend, including authentication token handling, real-time message propagation, and file upload/download workflows. Document the event-driven architecture patterns used for notifications and background processing. Include sequence diagrams showing typical user workflows like post creation, matching process, and chat communication. Address error propagation, retry mechanisms, and fallback strategies. Explain caching strategies, data synchronization patterns, and eventual consistency considerations across distributed components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-Time Chat & Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Real-Time Chat & Communication system focused on the instant messaging capabilities between users. Document the WebSocket-based real-time communication infrastructure, connection management, and message delivery mechanisms. Explain the conversation creation process, participant management, and message threading. Detail the chat interface components including message composition, real-time updates, and conversation history. Cover the integration with user authentication, ensuring secure messaging between verified users. Document the notification system for new messages, unread indicators, and message status tracking. Explain the backend services for message persistence, retrieval, and search functionality. Include examples of typical chat scenarios, conversation flows, and user interactions. Address privacy considerations, message retention policies, and moderation capabilities. Cover performance optimization for real-time messaging, connection scaling, and offline message handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Posts Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the user posts management page that allows users to view, edit, and delete their own posts. Document the user-specific post filtering, edit functionality for post details, and deletion controls with confirmation dialogs. Explain the status monitoring capabilities, showing post approval status, matching progress, and completion indicators. Detail the integration with the backend APIs for CRUD operations, real-time status updates, and user permission validation. Include the post editing workflow, image replacement functionality, and the approval status tracking. Provide examples of the user interface components, form handling for edits, and the complete user experience for managing their posted items throughout the approval and matching process.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the real-time messaging infrastructure using Supabase Realtime. Document the WebSocket connection setup, channel subscription management, and event handling for message synchronization. Explain the polling strategy for conversation updates, the hybrid approach combining polling and real-time updates, and conflict resolution mechanisms. Detail the optimistic UI implementation, local state management, and automatic synchronization with server state. Include examples of channel naming conventions, event filtering, and connection lifecycle management. Address error handling for connection failures, reconnection strategies, and graceful degradation when real-time features are unavailable.

wiki_catalogs.name: Password Reset and Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for password reset functionality and security measures in the MissLost authentication system. Detail the forgot password workflow including token generation, email verification, and security considerations to prevent email enumeration attacks. Document the reset password process with token validation, expiration handling, and secure password updates. Include concrete examples showing auth token management, token lifecycle, and password hashing with bcrypt. Explain the security measures implemented to protect against timing attacks, token replay attacks, and unauthorized access attempts. Address the revocation of refresh tokens upon password reset, token expiration policies, and best practices for secure password management and user communication.

wiki_catalogs.name: Approval Workflow and Moderation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Approval Workflow and Moderation system focused on content review and approval processes. Document the admin review interface, approval criteria, and decision-making workflows for both lost and found posts. Explain the status management system including pending, approved, rejected, and archived states with corresponding user notifications. Detail the moderation tools for administrators, including content review panels, user flagging mechanisms, and spam detection features. Cover the audit trail functionality for tracking all moderation actions and decisions. Document the integration with notification systems for informing users about post status changes. Explain the quality assurance measures, including duplicate detection, inappropriate content screening, and community guideline enforcement. Include examples of common moderation scenarios, decision trees, and administrative oversight procedures. Address appeal processes, escalation workflows, and administrative reporting capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema and Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the MissLost database schema. Detail entity relationships, field definitions, and data types for users, posts (lost/found), categories, AI matching records, chat conversations, and storage items. Document primary/foreign keys, indexes, and constraints including PostgreSQL-specific features like pg_trgm for text search and UUID generation. Explain data validation rules, business rules, and audit logging mechanisms. Include database schema diagrams and sample data structures. Document data access patterns, caching strategies, and performance considerations with PostgreSQL extensions. Specify data lifecycle, retention policies, and security measures including Row Level Security. Address data migration paths, trigger-based automatic updates, and version management for database schema evolution.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrative Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the administrative dashboard system. Document the admin overview statistics display, post management interface for content moderation, and user management controls. Explain the admin-only access patterns, role-based permissions, and administrative workflows. Detail the analytics dashboard components, content moderation tools, and user administration features. Include the admin navigation structure, data visualization components, and bulk operation capabilities. Provide examples of moderation workflows, user action logging, and administrative reporting features. Address security considerations for admin access and audit trail implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Storage & Handover API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for storage and handover endpoints. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, and request/response schemas for campus storage integration, item tracking, and handover process management. Include endpoints for storage allocation, item registration, handover approval workflows, and status tracking throughout the handover process. Document storage capacity management, item location tracking, and handover documentation requirements. Provide examples of storage workflows, handover procedures, and integration with the posts approval system.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Matching System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AI Matching System that powers intelligent post pairing. Thoroughly explain text similarity algorithms, YOLO-based object detection integration, and matching result management. Document the AI service functionality, similarity scoring mechanisms, and machine learning model integration. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing matching workflows, result ranking, and manual override capabilities. Document entity relationships with posts, user preferences, and matching history. Explain integration with PostgreSQL vector extensions, performance optimization strategies, and batch processing capabilities. Address common AI matching issues, accuracy tuning, and fallback mechanisms for low-confidence matches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Storage Management & Handover Process
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Storage Management & Handover Process focused on the physical item tracking and management system. Document the campus storage integration, including facility mapping, item location tracking, and inventory management. Explain the handover process workflow from post approval through item pickup/delivery, including scheduling, verification, and completion tracking. Detail the storage booking system, capacity management, and availability checking. Cover the integration with the post approval system and how storage availability affects item resolution. Document the administrative controls for storage management, capacity adjustments, and facility oversight. Explain the user interface for storage location selection, pickup scheduling, and handover confirmation. Include examples of typical handover scenarios, timing considerations, and logistics coordination. Address operational challenges, capacity planning, and peak usage management. Cover the integration with the training points system and reward mechanisms for successful handovers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Access Control and Guards
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for access control mechanisms and security guards in the MissLost authentication system. Explain JWT authentication guard implementation for protecting routes and ensuring user authentication. Document role-based access control using roles guard, role decorator usage, and permission management. Include concrete examples showing guard configuration, route protection patterns, and role-based authorization checks. Detail the public decorator functionality for bypassing authentication on specific routes. Address the integration between guards and NestJS dependency injection, guard execution order, and custom guard implementation patterns. Explain security considerations for route protection, role hierarchy management, and best practices for implementing fine-grained access control throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for all MissLost RESTful endpoints. For authentication APIs, document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and JWT token management for user registration, login, password recovery, and Google OAuth integration. For posts management APIs, document CRUD operations for lost and found posts including approval workflows, status management, and filtering capabilities. For AI matching APIs, document text similarity algorithms, YOLO-based categorization, and matching result endpoints. For chat APIs, document WebSocket connections, real-time messaging protocols, conversation management, and message handling. For storage APIs, document campus storage integration, item tracking, and handover process endpoints. Include authentication methods, error handling strategies, rate limiting, and versioning information. Document common use cases, client implementation guidelines, and performance optimization tips for each endpoint group.

wiki_catalogs.name: Supporting Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the reusable UI components that support the main application functionality. Explain the Header component with navigation and user controls, SideNavBar for main application navigation, AdminSideNavBar for administrative access, and CreatePostModal for post creation workflows. Detail the component props, event handling, styling patterns, and reusability across different pages. Include the modal component implementation, form handling within modals, and state management for user interactions. Provide examples of component composition, prop drilling patterns, and integration with the overall application state. Address accessibility considerations and responsive design patterns used in these components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Communication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Communication System encompassing real-time chat and notification services. Explain WebSocket-based messaging infrastructure, conversation management, and message persistence. Document the notification system architecture, event-driven notifications, and delivery mechanisms. Detail chat service functionality, message threading, and real-time updates. Include concrete examples of chat workflows, notification triggers, and user communication patterns. Document entity relationships between conversations, messages, and user profiles. Explain integration with Supabase real-time features, performance optimization for concurrent connections, and message queuing strategies. Address common communication issues, message delivery guarantees, and scaling considerations for real-time features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrative Dashboard & Oversight
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Administrative Dashboard & Oversight system focused on the administrative controls and content management capabilities. Document the admin overview dashboard with analytics, statistics, and system health monitoring. Explain the post management interface for content moderation, status updates, and user actions. Detail the user management system including role assignments, account controls, and user activity monitoring. Cover the administrative workflows for content review, policy enforcement, and system maintenance. Document the reporting and analytics features for tracking platform usage, engagement metrics, and community health indicators. Explain the integration with other system components for comprehensive oversight. Include examples of common administrative tasks, decision-making processes, and escalation procedures. Address the balance between automation and human oversight, including appeal processes and administrative discretion. Cover the security measures protecting administrative access and maintaining system integrity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration and Environment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive configuration documentation for the MissLost application environment setup. Document backend configuration including environment variables for Supabase credentials, JWT secrets, database connections, and AI service endpoints. Explain frontend configuration including Next.js settings, Supabase client initialization, API base URLs, and environment-specific optimizations. Detail Docker configuration for multi-container deployment including service dependencies, network setup, and volume mounting. Document security configurations including CORS policies, JWT token settings, and authentication guard implementations. Include production deployment considerations, environment variable management, and configuration validation. Provide examples for local development, staging, and production environments with specific variable values and setup procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration and Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for frontend configuration and setup. Document the Next.js configuration including optimization settings, build configurations, and environment variable handling. Explain the Supabase client initialization, API configuration patterns, and environment-specific settings. Detail the TypeScript configuration, path aliases, and compiler options. Include the build process, deployment configuration, and performance optimization settings. Provide examples of environment variable usage, API endpoint configuration, and client initialization patterns. Address development vs production configuration differences and troubleshooting common setup issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Storage and Handover System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Storage and Handover System that manages campus storage integration and item handover processes. Thoroughly explain storage entity management, item tracking, and physical location coordination. Document the handover service functionality, transaction management, and audit trail systems. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing storage allocation, item retrieval workflows, and handover completion processes. Document entity relationships with posts, users, and storage facilities. Explain integration with campus storage systems, capacity management, and scheduling coordination. Address common storage issues, inventory tracking problems, and handover verification processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Training Points & Recognition System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Training Points & Recognition System focused on the community engagement and reputation management mechanisms. Document the point allocation algorithms for various user activities including successful matches, helpful contributions, and community participation. Explain the trust score calculation methodology, point decay mechanisms, and recognition tiers. Detail the integration with user profiles, displaying trust scores, and awarding badges or special privileges. Cover the administrative controls for point adjustments, policy changes, and system maintenance. Document the user interface for point tracking, history viewing, and achievement displays. Explain the behavioral incentives driving positive community participation and encouraging responsible use of the platform. Include examples of point earning scenarios, milestone achievements, and community recognition programs. Address fairness considerations, point manipulation prevention, and appeals processes for disputed point allocations. Cover the relationship between trust scores and platform privileges, including access to advanced features and administrative tools.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Considerations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security documentation for the MissLost application. Document authentication and authorization mechanisms including JWT token security, role-based access control with admin/moderator permissions, and session management. Explain input validation strategies, SQL injection prevention through ORM usage, and data privacy measures including GDPR compliance considerations. Detail security middleware implementation including JWT authentication guards, role-based authorization guards, and exception handling for security-related errors. Document secure file upload handling, image processing security, and cloud storage access controls. Include security best practices for WebSocket connections, real-time chat security, and database access patterns. Address common security vulnerabilities, mitigation strategies, and security monitoring approaches for production deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Upload System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the File Upload System that handles image processing and cloud storage integration. Explain upload validation, file type restrictions, and size limitations. Document the upload service functionality, cloud storage integration, and image processing workflows. Detail the controller endpoints, multipart form handling, and response formatting. Include concrete examples of upload operations, error handling, and file management patterns. Explain integration with external storage services, security considerations for file uploads, and performance optimization strategies. Address common upload issues, validation failures, and storage quota management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing documentation for the MissLost application. Document unit testing strategies for backend services, controllers, and database operations using Jest framework. Explain integration testing approaches for API endpoints, database triggers, and external service integrations. Detail end-to-end testing procedures for complete user workflows including authentication, post creation, matching process, and chat functionality. Document frontend testing strategies including component testing, user interface validation, and integration testing with backend APIs. Include testing best practices, mock strategies for external services, and continuous integration setup. Provide examples of test cases, test data management, and automated testing pipelines. Address performance testing considerations, security testing approaches, and regression testing strategies for maintaining code quality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Triggers
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Database Triggers system. Detail trigger definitions, automatic update mechanisms, and audit logging functionality. Document PostgreSQL trigger implementation, permission management, and training points calculation triggers. Explain trigger relationships with core business logic, data integrity enforcement, and automated workflows. Include SQL trigger examples, migration scripts, and permission configurations. Document trigger service integration, event handling, and debugging approaches. Specify trigger lifecycle, performance impact, and maintenance procedures. Address trigger-related issues, debugging techniques, and optimization strategies for database performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines for the MissLost project. Document TypeScript coding standards, NestJS architecture patterns, and frontend component organization principles. Explain code formatting rules using Prettier, linting configurations with ESLint, and commit message conventions. Detail development workflow including branching strategies, code review processes, and continuous integration practices. Document best practices for database migrations, API versioning, and backward compatibility. Include guidelines for adding new features, extending existing modules, and maintaining code quality. Provide examples of proper error handling, logging strategies, and documentation standards. Address performance optimization techniques, security coding practices, and testing requirements for new contributions. Document the project's architectural principles and decision-making criteria for future development.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation for the MissLost application. Document common issues including authentication problems with JWT tokens and Google OAuth, database connection issues with PostgreSQL extensions, and performance optimization challenges. Explain debugging procedures using development tools, error analysis techniques, and log management strategies. Include frequently asked questions about installation problems, configuration errors, and runtime issues. Document solutions for Docker container networking problems, environment variable misconfigurations, and service dependency failures. Provide step-by-step resolution guides for common scenarios including failed migrations, authentication failures, and API connectivity issues. Address performance bottlenecks, memory optimization, and scaling considerations. Include monitoring and alerting strategies for production environments and maintenance procedures for database cleanup and system optimization.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# MissLost - Lost & Found Community Platform

## Project Introduction

**MissLost** is a comprehensive web platform designed specifically for the University of Economics and Technology (UEH) community to facilitate the reporting and recovery of lost and found personal items. The platform serves as a centralized digital solution addressing the common problem of misplaced belongings on university campuses.

### Purpose Statement
The system enables students, faculty, and staff to easily report lost items, discover found items, communicate through an integrated messaging system, and participate in a point-based recognition program for successful item returns.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Streamlined Reporting**: Simple interface for posting lost and found items with AI-assisted categorization
- **Community Connection**: Facilitate communication between item owners and finders
- **Administrative Oversight**: Provide admin tools for content moderation and system management
- **Recognition System**: Implement a training points system to encourage community participation
- **Campus Integration**: Connect with university storage facilities for item management

### Target Audience
- **Primary**: UEH students, faculty, and staff
- **Secondary**: University administration and storage facility personnel
- **Tertiary**: Community members seeking lost item recovery solutions

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modern full-stack architecture with clear separation between frontend and backend systems, utilizing microservice-like module organization within NestJS.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Frontend - Next.js] --> B[Backend - NestJS]
    B --> C[PostgreSQL Database]
    B --> D[Redis Cache]
    B --> E[Supabase Authentication]
    
    F[AI Detection] --> B
    G[Storage Management] --> B
    H[Chat System] --> B
    
    style A fill:#3B82F6
    style B fill:#10B981
    style C fill:#EF4444
    style D fill:#F59E0B
    style E fill:#8B5CF6
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer**
- **Next.js 16.2.3**: Modern React framework with server-side rendering
- **TypeScript**: Type-safe development across components
- **Tailwind CSS**: Utility-first styling with Material Design integration
- **Supabase Client**: Real-time database and authentication integration

**Backend Layer**
- **NestJS 11.0.1**: Enterprise Node.js framework with modular architecture
- **PostgreSQL 15+**: Primary database with advanced features (pg_trgm, vector extensions)
- **Redis**: Caching and session management
- **Supabase SSR**: Server-side authentication and database operations

**Integration Points**
- **Google OAuth**: Third-party authentication support
- **AI Detection**: YOLO-based object recognition for item categorization
- **Storage Integration**: Campus storage facility management
- **Real-time Communication**: WebSocket-enabled chat system

### Design Patterns

The architecture implements several key design patterns:

- **Module Pattern**: Each feature area (auth, posts, chat, etc.) encapsulated in dedicated modules
- **Repository Pattern**: Database operations abstracted through service layers
- **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable authentication strategies (JWT, Google OAuth)
- **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven notifications and real-time updates

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Bootstrap**
- [`main.ts`](backend/src/main.ts): Application initialization with CORS, validation pipes, and Swagger documentation
- [`app.module.ts`](backend/src/app.module.ts): Central module orchestrating all feature modules

**Frontend Shell**
- [`layout.tsx`](frontend/app/layout.tsx): Root layout with theme provider, route guard, and client shell wrapper

### Core Modules

**Authentication System**
- [`auth.module.ts`](backend/src/modules/auth/auth.module.ts): JWT and Google OAuth integration
- [`auth.service.ts`](backend/src/modules/auth/auth.service.ts): User registration, login, and token management
- [`jwt.strategy.ts`](backend/src/modules/auth/strategies/jwt.strategy.ts): JWT authentication strategy
- [`google.strategy.ts`](backend/src/modules/auth/strategies/google.strategy.ts): Google OAuth implementation

**Content Management**
- [`lost-posts.module.ts`](backend/src/modules/lost-posts/lost-posts.module.ts): Lost item posting functionality
- [`found-posts.module.ts`](backend/src/modules/found-posts/found-posts.module.ts): Found item posting functionality
- [`categories.module.ts`](backend/src/modules/categories/categories.module.ts): Item categorization system

**Communication System**
- [`chat.module.ts`](backend/src/modules/chat/chat.module.ts): Real-time messaging infrastructure
- [`notifications.module.ts`](backend/src/modules/notifications/notifications.module.ts): Event-driven notification system

### Configuration Approach

**Backend Configuration**
- [`supabase.config.ts`](backend/src/config/supabase.config.ts): Database connection management
- [`nest-cli.json`](backend/nest-cli.json): Build configuration and module resolution
- [`tsconfig.json`](backend/tsconfig.json): TypeScript compilation settings

**Frontend Configuration**
- [`next.config.ts`](frontend/next.config.ts): Next.js optimization and build configuration
- [`supabase.ts`](frontend/app/lib/supabase.ts): Frontend Supabase client initialization
- [`api.ts`](frontend/app/lib/api.ts): API client with automatic authentication

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies**
- **@nestjs/**: Core framework and ecosystem packages
- **@supabase/**: Database and authentication services
- **bcrypt**: Password hashing and verification
- **passport**: Authentication middleware
- **uuid**: Unique identifier generation

**Frontend Dependencies**
- **@supabase/supabase-js**: Real-time database operations
- **next**: React framework with server-side rendering
- **date-fns**: Date manipulation utilities
- **react**: Component library foundation

### Integration Points

**Database Schema Integration**
- [`OVERVIEW.md`](OVERVIEW.md): Complete PostgreSQL schema defining all tables, relationships, and constraints
- **Row Level Security**: PostgreSQL RLS policies for data access control
- **Full-text Search**: PostgreSQL extensions for efficient item searching

**AI Integration**
- **YOLO Detection**: Computer vision for automatic item categorization
- **Similarity Scoring**: AI-powered matching algorithm for lost-found pairs

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Auth Module] --> B[User Service]
    B --> C[JWT Strategy]
    B --> D[Google Strategy]
    
    E[Posts Module] --> F[Lost Posts Service]
    E --> G[Found Posts Service]
    F --> H[AI Matching Service]
    G --> H
    
    I[Chat Module] --> J[Message Service]
    I --> K[Conversation Service]
    
    L[Storage Module] --> M[Storage Items]
    N[Notifications] --> O[Event System]
    
    P[Upload Module] --> Q[File Storage]
    R[Triggers Module] --> S[Database Triggers]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**User Management System**
- Multi-role authentication (user, admin, storage_staff)
- Email verification and password reset workflows
- Profile management with training points tracking

**Item Posting System**
- Structured forms for lost and found items
- Image upload with AI-assisted categorization
- Status management (pending, approved, rejected, matched, closed)

**Smart Matching Algorithm**
- AI-powered item matching using YOLO object detection
- Text similarity scoring for description matching
- Manual override capabilities for administrators

**Communication Platform**
- Real-time messaging between parties
- Conversation management with read receipts
- System notifications for important events

**Administrative Tools**
- Content moderation dashboard
- User management interface
- Analytics and reporting capabilities

### Implementation Highlights

**AI-Powered Categorization**
- [`ai-matches.module.ts`](backend/src/modules/ai-matches/ai-matches.module.ts): Automated item matching system
- [`yolo_detection_logs`](OVERVIEW.md): AI detection logging and improvement tracking

**Storage Management**
- [`storage.module.ts`](backend/src/modules/storage/storage.module.ts): Campus storage integration
- [`storage_items`](OVERVIEW.md): Physical item tracking and management

**Real-time Communication**
- [`chat.module.ts`](backend/src/modules/chat/chat.module.ts): WebSocket-enabled messaging
- [`conversations`](OVERVIEW.md): Threaded conversation management

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Pending: New Post Created
    Pending --> Approved: Admin Review
    Pending --> Rejected: Admin Review
    Approved --> Matched: AI Matching
    Matched --> Closed: Successful Return
    Rejected --> [*]: Deletion
    
    state Matched {
        [*] --> OwnerConfirmed
        OwnerConfirmed --> FinderConfirmed
        FinderConfirmed --> Completed
        Completed --> [*]
    }
```

**Training Points System**
- [`training_point_logs`](OVERVIEW.md): Comprehensive point tracking and history
- Automated point allocation for successful item returns
- Point redemption and usage tracking

**Security and Compliance**
- Role-based access control with PostgreSQL RLS
- JWT-based authentication with refresh token rotation
- GDPR-compliant data handling and user consent management

The platform represents a comprehensive solution for campus community item recovery, combining modern web technologies with practical university-specific requirements while maintaining scalability and maintainability for future enhancements.

---

**Sources:**
- [README.md](README.md)
- [OVERVIEW.md](OVERVIEW.md)
- [package.json](backend/package.json)
- [package.json](frontend/package.json)
- [main.ts](backend/src/main.ts)
- [layout.tsx](frontend/app/layout.tsx)
- [app.module.ts](backend/src/app.module.ts)
- [auth.module.ts](backend/src/modules/auth/auth.module.ts)
- [supabase.config.ts](backend/src/config/supabase.config.ts)
- [next.config.ts](frontend/next.config.ts)
- [supabase.ts](frontend/app/lib/supabase.ts)
- [auth.service.ts](backend/src/modules/auth/auth.service.ts)
- [api.ts](frontend/app/lib/api.ts)
- [docker-compose.yml](docker-compose.yml)

</blog>
